GGplot2_DistVint_1set.Rmd

D. Grespin

June-2025

Load necessary packages

library(ggplot2)    # load high graphics plotting package
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(R.matlab)   # load MATLAB variable/structure reading package
## R.matlab v3.7.0 (2022-08-25 21:52:34 UTC) successfully loaded. See ?R.matlab for help.
## 
## Attaching package: 'R.matlab'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     getOption, isOpen
library(patchwork)  # Load gridding package for plotting side-by-side
library(scales)     # for more effective scaling
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

Read files in properly and create data frames

# read MATLAB .mat file into R (make sure to copy full path or place in your working directory)
distVintStruct <- readMat('/Users/drew.grespin/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UniversityofDenver/cav1_project/dataMats/track_dataMats/track24_forR_Mar25.mat')

# '/Users/drew.grespin/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UniversityofDenver/Jasper/Project II/5.22.25 L417 C57BL6 P7 CAV1 VECAD/Denoised 60x Leaf Bed/distVintStableRetina_May29.mat'

# Load raw data ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

distVintRaw     <- distVintStruct$track24.Mar25[[5]] # pull proper variable from MATLAB structure
dfRaw           <- data.frame(distVintRaw)    # convert object to data frame
# Set column names BEFORE using them

colnames(dfRaw) <- c('Normalized_Distance', 'Normalized_Intensity')

# Bin distances and take stratified sample


# Load interpolated data
distVintInterp     <- distVintStruct$track24.Mar25[[6]]  # pull proper variable from MATLAB structure
dfInterp           <- data.frame(distVintInterp)             # convert object to data frame
colnames(dfInterp) <- c('x', 'mean', 'sd', 'sem', 'weights') # change column names

# Load raw data ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Plotting Distance vs. Intensity ————————————————————————————

Plotting parameters

Scatter plot

scatterPlot  <- ggplot(data = dfRaw, mapping = aes(x = Normalized_Distance, y = Normalized_Intensity)) + 
                  geom_point(alpha = 0.05, color = "black", size = .25) # for circle: 0.05 alpha, size can be removed; for line: 0.25 alpha, size = 5;

scatterPlot2 <- scatterPlot +
                  theme_bw() +
                  theme(plot.title         = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'),  # main title
                        plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'),  # subtitle
                        plot.margin        = margin(t = 30, r = 40, b = 30, l = 40, unit = "pt"),
                        axis.title         = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'), 
                        axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 72, family = "Arial", margin = margin(t = 28), face = "bold"),
                        axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 72, family = "Arial", margin = margin(r = 5), face = "bold"),
                        panel.grid.major   = element_line(linewidth = 5, color = 'gray40'),
                        panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(linewidth = 2.5, color = 'gray40'),  
                        panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 2.5, color = 'gray40')) +
                  labs(title    = NULL,  # changes main title
                       subtitle = NULL,                      # changes subtitle
                       x        = NULL,          # changes x axis title
                       y        = NULL) +       # changes y axis title
                  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1),
                                     breaks = c( 0, 0.5, 1),
                                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25),   # keep minor ticks
                                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
                  scale_y_continuous(limits = c( 0, 1),
                                     breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1),
                                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25),   # keep minor ticks
                                     expand = c(0, 0))

Interpolated plot

# Creating interpolated plot
interpPlot  <- ggplot(dfInterp, aes(x = x, y = mean)) +
                  geom_line(color = "blue", size = 5) +
                  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean - sd, ymax = mean + sd), fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3)
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
interpPlot2 <- interpPlot +
                  theme_bw() +
                  theme(plot.title         = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'), # main title
                        plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'), # subtitle
                        plot.margin        = margin(t = 30, r = 40, b = 30, l = 40, unit = "pt"),
                        axis.title         = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'), 
                        axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 72, family = "Arial", margin = margin(t = 28), face = "bold"),
                        axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 72, family = "Arial", margin = margin(r = 5), face = "bold"),
                        panel.grid.major   = element_line(linewidth = 5, color = 'gray40'),
                        panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(linewidth = 2.5, color = 'gray40'),
                        panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 2.5, color = 'gray40')) +
                  labs(title    = NULL,  # changes main title
                       subtitle = NULL,  # changes subtitle
                       x        = NULL,          # changes x axis title
                       y        = NULL) +       # changes y axis title
                  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1),
                                     breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1),
                                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25),   # keep minor ticks
                                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
                  scale_y_continuous(limits = c( 0, 1),
                                     breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1),
                                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25),   # keep minor ticks
                                     expand = c(0, 0))

Scatter and interpolated plots

side by side

# fig.width = 14, fig.height = 7 is standard for paper
# fig.width = 19, fig.height = 7 is for circle in paper
# fig.width = 20.25, fig.height = 4.5 is for lines in paper
# fig.width = 20.25, fig.height = 9 is for crossbows in paper
# fig.width = 15.5, fig.height = 6 is standard for confluent lines in paper
# fig.width = 19, fig.height = 10 for taller ones

scatterPlot2 + interpPlot2
